property_description.description_of_prop.answer:
The advertisement is to be displayed at 142 Newman Drive, Newman WA 6753, which is a single-storey commercial tenancy operating as "Pilbara Outdoor & Camping Supplies" within a small neighborhood shopping strip. The property is situated on the eastern side of Newman Drive, approximately 150 metres south of the intersection with Fortescue Avenue, with on-site customer parking at the front and a service laneway at the rear.

The proposed sign will be positioned on the front street-facing elevation of the building, directly above the main entrance doors to the shop. It will be centered horizontally on the tenancy frontage to ensure symmetry with the shopfront glazing and entry, and to avoid conflict with existing awnings, downpipes, and security cameras. The bottom edge of the sign will be located just above the existing metal awning that projects over the footpath, leaving enough clearance for maintenance access and not interfering with any existing lighting or signage for neighboring tenancies.

More specifically, the sign will be mounted on the reinforced masonry wall panel that sits between the top of the glazed shopfront windows and the roof parapet. The mounting area is approximately 6.0 metres wide and 2.0 metres high; the sign will occupy a central portion of this panel with consistent margins to either side and above/below to maintain a balanced visual appearance. Fixings will be chem-set anchors into the structural masonry, concealed behind the sign panel to avoid visible brackets or rails.

The sign will face directly west toward Newman Drive so that it is clearly visible to vehicles travelling in both directions, as well as to pedestrians approaching from the on-site car park. It will not project beyond the existing title boundaries or over the public road reserve, and it will not obstruct sightlines to the existing crossover, parking bays, or the adjoining tenancy entrances. There are no overhead powerlines or street trees immediately in front of the proposed location, and the sign will be clear of the existing roof-mounted air-conditioning plant and associated ducting behind the parapet line.

In summary, the sign is to be fixed flush to the front façade, above the main entry doors, centred within the masonry panel, and fully contained within the property boundaries, designed to complement the building’s existing colour scheme and other approved signage within the complex.

details_of_proposed_sign.type_of_structu.answer: Wall mounted, non-projecting, rigid panel sign fixed flush to existing masonry façade above main entrance.

details_of_proposed_sign.colours_to_be_u.answer: Main background: deep charcoal grey (similar to Colorbond Monument). Primary text: white. Secondary text and tagline: light grey. Logo elements: burnt orange and muted sky blue. Supporting trim and border: matte black powder-coated frame. All colours selected to match existing building fascia and the Pilbara landscape aesthetic (reds and ochres).

details_of_proposed_sign.materials_to_be.answer: Sign panel: 3mm aluminium composite panel (ACP) with UV-stable, anti-graffiti laminate finish, designed for high temperature Pilbara conditions. Frame: 40mm x 40mm galvanised steel angle frame, powder-coated matte black, fixed to masonry wall. Fixings: stainless steel chem-set anchors and galvanised screws with neoprene washers to prevent corrosion. Graphics: high-quality, full-colour, UV-resistant vinyl print with protective overlaminate rated for 7+ years external use. All materials are non-reflective (low-gloss) to minimise glare to passing motorists and are designed to withstand high winds and dust typical of the region.

details_of_proposed_sign.state_the_inten.answer: N/A – the sign will not be illuminated. It will rely solely on existing ambient street lighting and the building’s existing external wall lights, with no dedicated light source attached to or directed at the sign.

advertising_period.period_of_time_for_wh.answer: Ongoing approval is sought for permanent business identification signage, intended to remain in place for the life of the current tenancy (anticipated minimum 5–10 years) or until replaced under a subsequent approved signage application.

existing_signs_to_be_removed.details_of_.answer:
There is currently a small, temporary, non-approved vinyl banner (approximately 0.6m high x 2.0m wide) fixed to the front awning using cable ties, displaying the words "OPEN 7 DAYS – CAMPING GEAR" in red text on a white background. This banner was installed as an interim measure when the business first opened and is visible from Newman Drive.

If this application is approved, the temporary banner will be completely removed, including all cable ties and any residual adhesive or fixings, prior to installation of the new permanent wall sign. No other existing permanent signage structures on the property will be removed or altered, and neighboring tenancies’ signage will remain unchanged.
